summary: ITER11 verification: Rule B/C debtor_tokens leak from iter10 is FIXED. server.py:519 introduces debtor_name_methods (strict: method=='debtor_name') used by Rule B (line 526-533) and Rule C (line 534-540); debtor_methods (broader: debtor_name OR debtor_tokens) retained only for PARTIAL reason text (line 566-577). PARTIAL reason now differentiates 'Token-substring match X%' vs 'Debtor name similarity X%'. All 14 iter10_overhaul tests PASS (incl. test_pass_2_5_debtor_tokens_low_confidence reproducer and test_six_row_canonical_scenario 4F/1P/1U with EMMERSON Rule C). All 18 prior regression tests (strict_classification + bearer_auth + auth_refresh) PASS. Total: 49/50 backend tests pass (98%). The one failing test is a stale pre-existing test unrelated to this fix.

minor.issue: STALE TEST — not a regression. Globex bank row scores 90 against 'Globex Industries Ltd' debtor; test expects status='full' but strict Rule B requires score>=95 (in place since iter9). Result is correctly partial/medium per spec. The test pre-dates the strict-classification overhaul and should be updated to expect 'partial'/'medium' (or use a higher-scoring fixture).

action_items: (LOW) Refresh /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py::test_fuzzy_matching_combined_reference_and_payer to align with current strict Rule B (score>=95) — either bump the fixture's debtor name similarity above 95 or assert partial/medium for the 90% Globex case. Not blocking the iter11 fix.
critical_code_review_comments: Iter11 fix is minimal and correct: 1 new variable (debtor_name_methods) used in Rule B and Rule C preconditions; broader debtor_methods retained only for PARTIAL branch reason text. Surgical, low risk.
PARTIAL reason text is now correctly differentiated: 'Token-substring match X%' for debtor_tokens (Pass 2.5) vs 'Debtor name similarity X%' for debtor_name (Pass 2). Aids reviewer triage.
Same long-standing recommendations from iter10 still apply: server.py is now 1118 lines — splitting matching engine into its own module would help.
Deprecation warnings on @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — should migrate to FastAPI lifespan handlers (non-blocking).

success_rate.backend: 98% (49/50 backend tests; the single failure is a stale unrelated test)
success_rate.frontend: Not re-tested — backend-only fix as specified by main agent

seed_data_creation: ~14 transient runs via POST /api/allocations during pytest (admin tenant). Not cleaned up; isolated to admin user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The Rule B/C debtor_tokens leak from iter10 is fully resolved as of iter11. All targeted reproducer tests pass. If the stale test_fuzzy_matching_combined_reference_and_payer is updated, the full backend suite will be 100% green. The matching engine code is structurally sound; future work should focus on (a) splitting server.py into modules and (b) migrating to FastAPI lifespan handlers.

rca_of_the_issue.iter10_bug: Rule B/C used debtor_methods (=[m for m in ms if m['method'] in ('debtor_name','debtor_tokens')]). Pass 2.5 token-substring matches (method='debtor_tokens', score=100 via partial_token_set_ratio) trivially passed Rule B's score>=95 gate => incorrect FULL.
rca_of_the_issue.iter11_fix: server.py:519 adds debtor_name_methods = [m for m in ms if m['method']=='debtor_name']. Rule B (line 526-533) and Rule C (line 534-540) now reference debtor_name_methods, structurally excluding debtor_tokens from FULL promotion. debtor_methods remains for PARTIAL reason construction only.
rca_of_the_issue.verification: test_pass_2_5_debtor_tokens_low_confidence (reproducer: 'XX9988 RANDOM NOISE FORD CIVIL JOBNO 12-A' £5400 + 'Ford Civil Engineering Construction Ltd' £5400) PASSES — bank row classified PARTIAL with reason 'Token-substring match 100% (low confidence — review required)'. test_six_row_canonical_scenario PASSES — 4 FULL / 1 PARTIAL / 1 UNMATCHED with EMMERSON Rule C firing as expected.
